methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)

data.narrative:
Open Cosmos is a Growth-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $50M to date. The sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, though none provide tight valuation anchors at Open Cosmos's stage and scale.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding $60M based on the $50M total raised as a proxy baseline. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar valuation tier) yielded three or more datapoints needed for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring cannot be deployed because Open Cosmos's revenue is not on file; the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 49.8x P/S across the five public tickers, but applying that multiple requires disclosed revenue.

Given the single applicable method, the analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, centered on $60M. This range reflects uncertainty bounds rather than methodological triangulation. Key uncertainties include Open Cosmos's revenue run-rate (needed for public-comp anchoring), the absence of same-stage private comparables, and whether the 1.2x stage multiplier appropriately reflects frontier-aerospace risk and growth profile. A more granular valuation would require either disclosed financial metrics or access to recent financings of closer-stage peers.
